text:
User: don't show PTY label -- don't show working or idle or stuff like that label -- use a little badge indicator color coded -- deterministically color workspace name too -- wherever we show stuff in the console that shows a workspace name we should always color code deterministically with the same function (consistent color per project) [...] User: pty is not a state -- the agent is either actively in a loop (busy) or it reached end turn (idle)

ok -- implement [...] Where:

- `●` is the color-only state badge—no `working`, `idle`, or `PTY` text. [...] User: pty is not a state -- the agent is either actively in a loop (busy) or it reached end turn (idle)

ok -- implement

Assistant: The design is now decided: the badge represents only loop state—busy or idle. PTY is transport metadata used solely to determine whether Enter can attach; it is never rendered as state. I’m closing the exploration, promoting it to the GitHub queue, and implementing from an isolated worktree.
